content: DSM-5-TR includes 'with peripartum onset' specifier for depressive/bipolar episodes: onset during pregnancy or within 4 weeks postpartum (though clinically, postpartum period often extended to 12 months). Postpartum depression meets criteria for Major Depressive Disorder with peripartum specifier. Key features may include: mood lability, tearfulness, anxiety about infant care, intrusive thoughts about harm to infant (distinguish from psychotic commands), difficulty bonding. Postpartum 'blues' (50-80% of mothers): mild mood symptoms days 3-5 postpartum, self-limited, doesn't meet depression criteria. Postpartum psychosis: rapid onset mood symptoms (mania/depression), delusions (often infant-related), hallucinations, disorganization, insomnia; requires immediate evaluation as psychiatric emergency.
